message:
fix(indexer): mark exact totals on skipped post-full-scan rebuild + extract record_exact_total_counts_in_progress helper

When `should_skip_post_full_scan_authoritative_rebuild` returns true
we stop calling the DB rebuild, but the old code was using that
rebuild path as the sole place that set
`stats.total_counts_exact = true` and wrote the authoritative
conversation/message counts into the live progress stats. As a
result, a repeat `cass index --full --json` on an unchanged corpus
successfully skipped the redundant rebuild but then emitted
`total_counts_exact: false` with stale/scan-phase counts. Clients
using the JSON payload (e.g. the viewer, the flywheel dashboard)
would see the "exact" flag flip to false on every no-op refresh.

Fix:

- Extract the stats-writing pattern (`if let Some(progress) ... if
  let Ok(mut stats) = progress.stats.lock() { stats.total_* = ...;
  stats.total_counts_exact = true; }`) into a small free function
  `record_exact_total_counts_in_progress(progress, total_conversations,
  total_messages)` that does the Option+Mutex dance in one place. This
  lets us share the same invariant (`total_counts_exact` must go to
  true whenever we write authoritative totals) across every site that
  knows an exact count.
- Replace the four existing inline writes in `run_index` with calls
  to the helper: the initial pre-scan rebuild path, the
  canonical-only-full-rebuild path, the forced-rebuild path inside
  the staged-rebuild branch, and the post-scan rebuild path.
- In the skip branch of the new
  `should_skip_post_full_scan_authoritative_rebuild` predicate, read
  the storage directly via `count_total_conversations_exact` +
  `count_total_messages_exact` and thread them through the same
  helper. These counts were already read in the rebuild branch as
  `rebuild_convs`; reading them again in the skip branch is cheap
  (two SQL count queries on already-open sqlite) and gives the
  progress stats the exact totals the JSON payload needs.

Regression test (`tests/cli_index.rs`):

- `repeat_full_json_marks_exact_totals_when_post_scan_rebuild_is_skipped`
  runs `cass index --full` twice against a seeded codex session and
  asserts that the *second* full run's JSON payload reports
  `indexing_stats.total_counts_exact == true`, and that the
  `conversations` / `messages` top-level numbers match
  `indexing_stats.total_conversations` / `total_messages`. Before the
  fix this assertion fails: the skipped-rebuild path leaves
  `total_counts_exact` false on the no-op refresh.
